summary: Iter 93 - Homebrew Workshop + Custom Ability Score mode. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_iter93_homebrew_custom.py — covers parse-text (Pirate background w/ Athletics+Perception, race w/ size+speed in draft or missing, invalid content_type→400, empty→400), parse-docx (in-memory python-docx Pirate.docx → draft populated, .pdf rejected with 400), save (uuid id returned, update with same homebrew_id reuses id, no _id leak), list (all 5 buckets — race/class/subclass/background/magic_item — present + filter by content_type works), delete (200 with deleted={id}, second delete→404), auth guards (all 5 endpoints return 401/403 unauthenticated), and light regression (login + admin/check + GET /api/characters). Frontend: full Homebrew Workshop walkthrough succeeded — login → homebrew-workshop-btn on /home → /homebrew → all 5 type tabs (race/class/subclass/background/magic_item) + hb-edition + hb-paste-text + hb-parse-text-btn render → parse btn label flips to 'Parsing…' → hb-draft-editor populates → hb-save-btn creates hb-item-{uuid} in library → hb-edit-{id} reopens draft → hb-delete-{id} → 'Deleted' toast → item gone (count 1→0). Static races (Human, Elf, Dwarf, Halfling, Gnome, Half-Orc, Half-Elf, Tiefling, Aasimar, Goliath, Orc) render on Step 2/Race. AbilitiesStep custom mode verified by code review (live walkthrough blocked on race-card click selector — see context note).
critical_code_review_comments: homebrew.py is solid: pydantic input validation, content_type whitelist (5 types), per-content-type required-fields map for missing_fields, _extract_json gracefully handles ```json fences AND raw JSON, async LLM call with try/except → 502 on AI fail, idempotent ruleset bucket lookup ('Homebrew Workshop' per-edition), and consistent _id stripping on every response. Auth-gated via Depends(get_current_user) on all 5 routes.
Save endpoint uses uuid4() for new ids and reuses homebrew_id on update — verified PASS in test_full_lifecycle (same id returned on second save).

AbilitiesStep.js custom mode (lines 524-588) is clean: stat-dec disables at ≤1, stat-inc disables at ≥20, number input clamped to 1-20 in onChange via Math.max(1, Math.min(20, parseInt)). MIN_ABILITY_SCORE in characterRules.js correctly lowered to 1.

HomebrewWorkshop.js has every required data-testid (hb-type-{race|class|subclass|background|magic_item}, hb-edition, hb-paste-text, hb-parse-text-btn, hb-draft-editor, hb-save-btn, hb-item-{id}, hb-edit-{id}, hb-delete-{id}); button label correctly toggles to 'Parsing…' during async LLM call.
Minor (informational only): parse-docx route also accepts .txt and .md as a convenience — not in problem statement but graceful and harmless. Non-.docx (e.g. .pdf) is correctly rejected with 400.

success_rate.frontend: Homebrew Workshop end-to-end PASS (parse → save → edit → delete → toast). Race step PASS (all 11 static races visible). Custom Ability mode verified by code review only — live walkthrough timed out on race-card click selector (see context_for_next_testing_agent).

seed_data_creation: test_full_lifecycle creates a TEST_iter93_Pirate_<hex> homebrew background and deletes it within the same test (no leftover seed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter93 backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iter93_homebrew_custom.py — re-run with `pytest -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iter93_results.xml`. The Homebrew Workshop end-to-end UI flow (parse-text → draft → save → library → edit → delete) is fully verified including the LLM round-trip (real Claude Sonnet returned a populated Pirate draft within ~10s). Custom Ability Score mode walkthrough was blocked because the Race-step card uses a complex layout where `h3:has-text("Human")` times out even though the card is clearly visible — likely the h3 is inside a clickable parent <div> with pointer-events:none on the heading itself. To reach Step 5 next time, click by `[role="button"]:has-text("Human")` or click the parent card via `.has(h3:text("Human"))` ancestor selector, or set localStorage characterDraft directly. The data-testids, methods, and disabled-state logic for Custom mode are correct per code review (AbilitiesStep.js lines 285-295 for the 4 method buttons, 326 for custom-mode-info, 524-588 for the 1-20 input and steppers).
rca of the issue: No bugs found — Homebrew Workshop and Custom Ability Score modes are implemented correctly. The only test friction was a Playwright selector against the Race-step cards; functionality is unaffected and confirmed via code review.
